topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an oppressive, irritating drone, mirroring the frantic thumping in Alex’s chest. He stared at the screen, the numbers stark and unforgiving. Sarah’s latest performance review, a document he’d accidentally opened while looking for a shared file, displayed a salary figure that made his stomach clench. It was a significant gap, a chasm separating his current compensation from what Sarah was earning. A cold sweat prickled his hairline. He gripped the edge of his desk so tightly his knuckles turned white. Every email, every late night spent perfecting a report, suddenly felt hollow, a pointless expenditure of energy. He took a shaky breath, the air tasting thin in his lungs, and slowly, deliberately, he minimized the window, trying to force the image away. He had to keep moving, had to keep pretending everything was fine, even though his hands were trembling too much to type.

[story 2]

"Just another Tuesday," Mark muttered, the words tasting like ash in his mouth. He kept glancing over at Chloe, who was effortlessly charming in the breakroom, laughing easily while discussing weekend plans. Mark felt a profound sense of inadequacy settle over him, heavy and suffocating. He reread the spreadsheet for the fifth time, the figures mocking him. Chloe’s compensation was listed right next to his own, a stark, undeniable contrast that felt like a physical blow. He stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor. His jaw was tight, clenched so hard he could feel the strain in his neck. He needed a plan, something immediate, something drastic. He walked toward the water cooler, his movements stiff and jerky, needing the cool plastic of the bottle to ground him before the churning anxiety inside him threatened to spill over.

[story 3]

Elena traced the lines of the pay stub with a fingertip, the paper feeling flimsy and worthless beneath her touch. The silence in her small cubicle seemed magnified, amplifying the frantic rhythm of her pulse. When she finally looked up, her eyes were wide, reflecting the harsh glow of her monitor. The disparity between her take-home pay and that of young Ben was a gaping wound. A tightness constricted her throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. She started tapping her pen against the desk repeatedly, a rapid, uneven beat that she tried to mask with focused concentration on a trivial spreadsheet. She felt a consuming urge to either confess the discrepancy or simply disappear. Her fingers hovered over the 'send' button for an internal message, the weight of the potential fallout pressing down on her shoulders, making her entire body feel heavy and profoundly unsteady.
